## Overview

The ui-ux-pro-max-skill is an AI skill that provides design intelligence for building professional UI/UX across multiple platforms and frameworks.

- **Repository URL**: https://github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill

## Key Features

The flagship feature of v2.0 is the Design System Generator - an AI-powered reasoning engine that analyzes your project requirements and generates a complete, tailored design system in seconds.

The plugin features a knowledge base with 57 UI styles, 95 color palettes, 56 font combinations, and works as a skill/workflow for AI coding assistants.

## Supported Platforms

The tool supports HTML/Tailwind, React, Next.js, Vue, Nuxt.js, Nuxt UI, Svelte, ShadCN, Flutter, SwiftUI, React Native, and Jetpack Compose

## How It Works

Users can request UI/UX tasks, and the AI automatically generates a complete design system, finds the best matching styles, colors, and typography based on product type and requirements, and implements the UI with proper colors, fonts, spacing, and best practices.

```text
# UI UX Pro Max - AI Design Intelligence Skill

## Overview

UI UX Pro Max is an AI skill providing design system intelligence for building professional interfaces across multiple platforms and frameworks. It features 67 UI styles, 96 color palettes, 57 font pairings, and 100 industry-specific reasoning rules.

## Key Features

**Design Assets:**
- "67 UI Styles" ranging from minimalism to cyberpunk aesthetics
- "96 Color Palettes" organized by industry (SaaS, healthcare, fintech, beauty)
- "57 Font Pairings" with Google Fonts integration
- 25 chart type recommendations for dashboards
- 13 supported tech stacks

**Intelligent Generation:**
The v2.0 flagship feature is an automated design system generator using reasoning engines that analyze project requirements and generate complete design systems in seconds.

**Industry Coverage:**
Rules tailored for tech, finance, healthcare, e-commerce, services, creative fields, and emerging tech sectors.

## Installation

### Via CLI (Recommended)

```bash
npm install -g uipro-cli
cd /path/to/your/project
uipro init --ai claude    # Claude Code
uipro init --ai cursor    # Cursor
uipro init --ai windsurf  # Windsurf
uipro init --ai all       # All assistants
```

### Via Claude Marketplace

```
/plugin marketplace add nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
/plugin install ui-ux-pro-max@ui-ux-pro-max-skill
```

### Prerequisites

Python 3.x is required for the search script functionality.

## Usage Modes

**Skill Mode (Auto-activate):**
Supported in Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, and others. Simply request UI/UX work naturally in chat.

**Workflow Mode (Slash Command):**
For Kiro, GitHub Copilot, and Roo Code:
```
/ui-ux-pro-max Build a landing page for my SaaS product
```

## Supported Tech Stacks

Web frameworks include React, Next.js, Vue, Nuxt.js, Svelte, and Astro. Mobile: SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose, React Native, Flutter. Default: HTML + Tailwind.

## Advanced Features

**Design System Generation:**
Command-line access to reasoning engine for domain-specific searches and design system persistence using a Master + Overrides pattern for hierarchical retrieval across sessions.

**Persistence Structure:**
```
design-system/
â”śâ”€â”€ MASTER.md           # Global source of truth
â””â”€â”€ pages/
    â””â”€â”€ [page-name].md  # Page-specific overrides
```

## Support & Resources

- Repository: github.com/nextlevelbuilder/ui-ux-pro-max-skill
- Website: uupm.cc
- 35.9k GitHub stars, 3.5k forks
- Includes 99 UX guidelines and anti-pattern documentation
```

# CLAUDE.md Content Summary

## Overview
This file guides Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with the "Antigravity Kit" â€“ an AI-powered design intelligence toolkit. It provides searchable databases of UI styles, color palettes, font pairings, chart types, and UX guidelines functioning as a skill/workflow for AI coding assistants.

## Search Command Syntax
```
python3 src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --domain <domain> [-n <max_results>]
```

**Available domains:** product, style, typography, color, landing, chart, ux

**Stack-specific search:**
```
python3 src/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --stack <stack>
```

Supported stacks include html-tailwind (default), react, nextjs, astro, vue, nuxtjs, nuxt-ui, svelte, swiftui, react-native, flutter, shadcn, and jetpack-compose.

## Architecture
The source of truth resides in `src/ui-ux-pro-max/`. The system uses symlinks for `.claude/`, `.factory/`, and `.shared/` directories. The search engine combines BM25 ranking with regex matching, supporting automatic domain detection when `--domain` is omitted.

## Sync Rules
- **Edit data & scripts** in `src/ui-ux-pro-max/`; changes propagate automatically via symlinks
- **Update templates** in `src/ui-ux-pro-max/templates/` (base/ and platforms/)
- **Before publishing CLI**, copy assets: `cp -r src/ui-ux-pro-max/{data,scripts,templates}/* cli/assets/`

## Prerequisites
Requires Python 3.x with no external dependencies.

## Git Workflow
Never push directly to `main`. Instead: create a branch (`feat/...` or `fix/...`), commit, push, then submit a PR via `gh pr create`.
```

```text
# UI/UX Pro Max Skill Overview

## Core Purpose
This skill provides comprehensive design intelligence for web and mobile applications, offering "50+ styles, 97 color palettes, 57 font pairings, 99 UX guidelines, and 25 chart types across 9 technology stacks."

## How It Operates

**Primary Workflow:**
1. Analyze user requirements (product type, style, industry, tech stack)
2. Generate design system using `--design-system` flag (REQUIRED step)
3. Supplement with domain-specific searches as needed
4. Apply stack-specific guidelines
5. Implement with pre-delivery checklist

**Key Command Structure:**
```bash
python3 skills/ui-ux-pro-max/scripts/search.py "<query>" --design-system [-p "Project Name"]
```

## Eight Priority Categories

The skill organizes guidance by impact level:

| Priority | Category | Impact |
|----------|----------|--------|
| 1-2 | Accessibility, Touch/Interaction | CRITICAL |
| 3-4 | Performance, Layout/Responsive | HIGH |
| 5-6 | Typography, Animation | MEDIUM |
| 7-8 | Style Selection, Charts | MEDIUM-LOW |

## Searchable Domains

Users can target specific knowledge areas: `product`, `style`, `typography`, `color`, `landing`, `chart`, `ux`, `react`, `web`, and `prompt`.

## Activation Triggers

Use when designing UI components, choosing colors/fonts, reviewing code for UX issues, building dashboards/landing pages, or implementing accessibility requirements.

## Notable Feature: Persistent Design Systems

The `--persist` flag creates hierarchical documentation: a master design system with optional page-specific overrides, enabling consistent multi-page projects.
```
</details></tool-use>

    70â†’## 3. Spacing, Radius & Geometry
    71â†’
    72â†’**Spacing System (8-Point Grid)**
    73â†’
    74â†’* `2px` (Minor) â€” Gap between grouped monospace data items.
    75â†’* `4px` (Tight) â€” Padding inside small interactive toggles or input boundaries.
    76â†’* `8px` (Base) â€” Margins parsing list items within the Theory Inspector.
    77â†’* `16px` (Default) â€” Standard padding for Surface Cards and Modal dialogues.
    78â†’* `32px` (Macro) â€” Spatial separation between Sandbox and Sidebar.
    79â†’
    80â†’**Border Radius System**
    81â†’
    82â†’* `0px` (Sharp) â€” Edges connected to the VexFlow canvas, red logic lines, and algorithmic constraints.
    83â†’* `2px` (Micro) â€” Tooltips and metadata pills.
    84â†’* `4px` (Standard) â€” Interactive buttons, overrides, and toggles (signifying human touch).
    85â†’* `50%` (Circular) â€” Singularly reserved for the draggable noteheads.
    86â†’
    87â†’---
    88â†’
    89â†’## 4. Layout & Responsiveness
    90â†’
    91â†’To ensure the Glass-box UI degrades gracefully while preserving the VexFlow canvas:
    92â†’
    93â†’* **Breakpoint Constraints:**
    94â†’  * `lg` (â‰Ą1024px): 70% Sandbox / 30% Theory Inspector (Side-by-Side).
    95â†’  * `md` (768px - 1023px): 60% Sandbox / 40% Theory Inspector.
    96â†’  * `sm` (<768px): Stacked. The `InteractiveTactileSandbox` takes 100% width. The `TheoryInspectorSidebar` becomes a bottom-anchored pull-up drawer (BottomSheet) triggered by selection.
    97â†’* **VexFlow Scaling:** The VexFlow SVG target `div` must NOT use fixed pixel widths. It must use `w-full h-full` and rely on a `ResizeObserver` hook to trigger a re-render of `Vex.Flow.Factory` when container dimensions change.
    98â†’* **Typography Scaling:** Use CSS `clamp()` for headings to prevent horizontal overflow in Inspector panels.
    99â†’
   100â†’---
   101â†’
   102â†’## 5. Motion & Interaction Rules
   103â†’
   104â†’* **Note Drag Behavior:** Notes move discretely via a snapping coordinate system corresponding to semitones/diatonic steps. Continuous, fluid dragging (analog-style physical interpolation) is forbidden to enforce strict symbolic logic.
   105â†’* **Violation Reveal Timing:** Zero latency. Red Line systems appear simultaneously with the drop event/keyboard confirmation. No "fade-in" animations are permitted.
   106â†’* **Repair Transitions:** When switching between user state and recommended repair state, employ a crisp `0ms` swap. Linear interpolation (`100ms max`) reserved strictly for programmatic voice-leading paths in SVG nodes only.
   107â†’* **Forbidden Animation:** No bouncing, spring physics, or easing functions that imply weight or physical realism.
   108â†’
   109â†’---
   110â†’
   111â†’## 6. Iconography System
   112â†’
   113â†’* **Style:** Sharp, linear, 1.5px stroke weight (2px on hover). Filled icons are explicitly forbidden unless representing active global state booleans.
   114â†’* **Visual Metaphors:** Avoid consumer UI metaphors (no "magic wands" for generation). Use drafting and mechanical metaphors (e.g., compasses, set squares, logic gates).
   115â†’* **Semantic Geometry:**
   116â†’  * **Sharp Angles (Triangles/Rectangles):** Denote algorithmic rules, calculations, and constraints.
   117â†’  * **Curves/Circles:** Denote user input, human interaction, or override functions.
   118â†’
   119â†’---
   120â†’
   121â†’## 7. Accessibility, Operability, Content & Voice (POUR)
   122â†’
   123â†’**1. Perceivable & Operable**
   124â†’
   125â†’* **Color Independence:** Semantic color cues must always be paired with secondary visual signifiers (e.g., solid line vs. dashed line).
   126â†’* **Edit-Authority via Keyboard:** Full edit-authority of the VexFlow state must be possible via Arrow and Modifier keys without a pointer device. Tab sequences must fluidly navigate between the Sandbox and Inspector layers.
   127â†’
   128â†’**2. Understandable & Robust**
   129â†’
   130â†’* **Screen Reader Handling:** Draggable Note Tokens inject ARIA labels formatted as `[Voice]: [Pitch] [Duration]` (e.g., `Soprano: C5 Quarter Note`).
   131â†’* **Live Regions for Causality:** Rule violations announce immediately as aria-live critical alerts: `Alert: Parallel Fifth detected between Soprano and Tenor`.
   132â†’* **Semantic DOM Elements:** SVG interaction must map to standard, accessible HTML `<button>` and `<input>` proxy elements within the React application tree.
   133â†’
   134â†’**3. Content & Voice Guidelines**
   135â†’
   136â†’* **Tone:** Scholarly, objective, calm, and non-judgmental. Do not use conversational filler or emotional validation.
   137â†’* **AI Explanations:** Confined strictly to the Theory Inspector panel to prevent cluttering the Sandbox. Use precise language like "Stylistic Observation: Doubled Leading Tone."
   138â†’
   139â†’---
   140â†’
   141â†’## 8. Developer Hand-off & Schema Definition
   142â†’
   143â†’To effectively generate production-ready React/VexFlow code without hallucination, all components and logic must adhere to the following contracts.
   144â†’
   145â†’### A. State Management & Data Ontology (Zustand)
   146â†’
   147â†’The core of the application must rely on a strictly typed, immutable representation of musical state.
   148â†’
   149â†’```typescript
   150â†’// types/music.ts
   151â†’
   152â†’/**
   153â†’ * Employs the "Theory Named" strategy. 
   154â†’ * Pitches must be explicitly defined using scientific pitch notation.
   155â†’ */
   156â†’export type PitchClass = 'C' | 'C#' | 'D' | 'D#' | 'E' | 'F' | 'F#' | 'G' | 'G#' | 'A' | 'A#' | 'B';
   157â†’export type Octave = 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 6 | 7;
   158â†’export type Duration = 'w' | 'h' | 'q' | '8' | '16'; // VexFlow compatible
   159â†’
   160â†’export interface NoteEvent {
   161â†’  id: string; // Unique UUID
   162â†’  pitch: `${PitchClass}${Octave}`; 
   163â†’  duration: Duration;
   164â†’  voice: 'Soprano' | 'Alto' | 'Tenor' | 'Bass';
   165â†’  isUserModified: boolean; // Triggers UI 'Accent' color states
   166â†’}
   167â†’
   168â†’export interface TheoryViolation {
   169â†’  id: string;
   170â†’  ruleId: string; // e.g., 'RULE_PARALLEL_FIFTH'
   171â†’  severity: 'Violation' | 'Warning';
   172â†’  involvedNoteIds: string[]; // Maps directly to Red Line SVGs
   173â†’  isOverridden: boolean;
   174â†’}
   175â†’
   176â†’// store/useScoreStore.ts
   177â†’export interface ScoreState {
   178â†’  notes: NoteEvent[];
   179â†’  violations: TheoryViolation[];
   180â†’  selectedNoteId: string | null;
   181â†’  moveNote: (id: string, newPitch: `${PitchClass}${Octave}`) => void;
   182â†’  toggleOverride: (violationId: string) => void;
   183â†’}
   184â†’```
   185â†’
   186â†’### B. VexFlow / React Boundary Constraints
   187â†’
   188â†’Direct manipulation of the VexFlow canvas via virtual DOM updates is forbidden.
   189â†’
   190â†’* **The Rule of the Wrapper:** VexFlow rendering logic must be isolated within a `useEffect` hook that triggers re-renders *only* when the symbolic `ScoreState` changes or container resizes.
   191â†’* **Proxy Elements for POUR:** Invisible HTML `<button>` elements must be positioned absolutely over the VexFlow note coordinates to handle `onFocus`, `onKeyDown`, and `onClick` synthetic events, dispatching updates to Zustand.
   192â†’
   193â†’```typescript
   194â†’// components/organisms/StaffRenderer.tsx
   195â†’// Required implementation pattern
   196â†’
   197â†’export const StaffRenderer: React.FC = () => {
   198â†’  const containerRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);
   199â†’  const notes = useScoreStore((state) => state.notes);
   200â†’
   201â†’  useEffect(() => {
   202â†’    if (!containerRef.current) return;
   203â†’    
   204â†’    // 1. Clear previous canvas
   205â†’    containerRef.current.innerHTML = '';
   206â†’    
   207â†’    // 2. Initialize VexFlow Formatter
   208â†’    const vf = new Vex.Flow.Factory({
   209â†’      renderer: { elementId: containerRef.current, width: containerRef.current.clientWidth, height: 200 }
   210â†’    });
   211â†’    
   212â†’    // 3. Map strictly typed NoteEvents to Vex.Flow.StaveNote
   213â†’    // ... Imperative VexFlow logic here ...
   214â†’    
   215â†’    vf.draw();
   216â†’  }, [notes]); // Re-render strictly bounded to state determinism AND resize events
   217â†’
   218â†’  return <div ref={containerRef} className="relative w-full h-full" />;
   219â†’};
   220â†’```
   221â†’
   222â†’### C. React Component Library (Atomic Specification)
   223â†’
   224â†’LLMs must implement the following Prop interfaces exactly as defined. Do not generate arbitrary local state; all core logic resides in Zustand.
   225â†’
   226â†’**Atoms**
   227â†’
   228â†’```typescript
   229â†’interface SemanticLabelProps {
   230â†’  type: 'Violation' | 'Warning' | 'Override' | 'Valid';
   231â†’  text: string;
   232â†’  className?: string;
   233â†’}
   234â†’
   235â†’interface ToggleSwitchBaseProps {
   236â†’  checked: boolean;
   237â†’  onChange: (checked: boolean) => void;
   238â†’  ariaLabel: string;
   239â†’  disabled?: boolean;
   240â†’}
   241â†’```
   242â†’
   243â†’**Molecules**
   244â†’
   245â†’```typescript
   246â†’interface DraggableNoteTokenProps {
   247â†’  id: string; // References NoteEvent UUID
   248â†’  pitch: string;
   249â†’  isFocused: boolean;
   250â†’  hasError: boolean;
   251â†’  onMove: (newPitch: string) => void;
   252â†’}
   253â†’
   254â†’interface TheoryViolationIndicatorProps {
   255â†’  id: string;
   256â†’  severity: 'Violation' | 'Warning';
   257â†’  points: { x: number; y: number }[]; // Computed BBox from VexFlow
   258â†’  isOverridden: boolean;
   259â†’}
   260â†’
   261â†’interface ConstraintControlProps {
   262â†’  currentLevel: 'Strict' | 'Standard' | 'Loose';
   263â†’  onLevelChange: (level: 'Strict' | 'Standard' | 'Loose') => void;
   264â†’}
   265â†’```
   266â†’
   267â†’**Organisms**
   268â†’
   269â†’```typescript
   270â†’interface TheoryExplanationCardProps {
   271â†’  violationId: string;
   272â†’  ruleId: string;
   273â†’  severity: 'Violation' | 'Warning';
   274â†’  academicDescription: string; // Scholarly tone enforced
   275â†’  isOverridden: boolean;
   276â†’  onOverrideToggle: (violationId: string) => void;
   277â†’}
   278â†’```
   279â†’
   280â†’---
   281â†’
   282â†’## 9. Music Theory Rendering Axioms
   283â†’
   284â†’To utilize the **"Theory Named" strategy** effectively, the UI visually translates axiomatic music logic under these constraints:
   285â†’
   286â†’1. **Pitch vs. Spelling Strictness:** The system must differentiate between enharmonic equivalents (e.g., C# vs. Db). Interpreting intervals based on MIDI note numbers is forbidden. VexFlow must receive the exact theoretical spelling from the Backend.
   287â†’2. **Violation Coordinates (The "Red Line" Tooltips):** When drawing the `TheoryViolationIndicator` molecule over VexFlow output, the bounding box of the specific `StaveNote` instances involved must be calculated. The Red Line is an SVG overlay using React portal logic relative to the `containerRef`, ensuring decoupling from VexFlow computation.
   288â†’3. **Non-Blocking Edits:** The handler for a `moveNote` action must inherently accept *any* pitch modification. The UI renders the new pitch instantly, and evaluating constraints follows afterwards. The UI must never reject a move action defensively (Expressive Sovereignty).
   289â†’
   290â†’---
   291â†’
   292â†’## 10. Design Tokens (JSON Base)
   293â†’
   294â†’The system leverages hierarchical token mapping via JSON, easily compiled to Tailwind CSS theme schemas.
   295â†’
   296â†’```json
   297â†’{
   298â†’  "harmonyForge": {
   299â†’    "color": {
   300â†’      "sonata": {
   301â†’        "bg": "#FDF5E6",
   302â†’        "surface": "#9E4B3E",
   303â†’        "detail": "#D2B48C",
   304â†’        "accent": "#FFB300"
   305â†’      },
   306â†’      "nocturne": {
   307â†’        "bg": "#3E2723",
   308â†’        "surface": "#A55B37",
   309â†’        "detail": "#2D1817",
   310â†’        "accent": "#FFB300"
   311â†’      },
   312â†’      "semantic": {
   313â†’        "violation": "#D32F2F",
   314â†’        "warning": "#1976D2",
   315â†’        "override": "#7B1FA2",
   316â†’        "valid": "#D2B48C"
   317â†’      }
   318â†’    },
   319â†’    "typography": {

```text
## UI Pro Max Search Results
**Domain:** ux | **Query:** accessibility keyboard navigation canvas editor
**Source:** ux-guidelines.csv | **Found:** 5 results

### Result 1
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Keyboard Navigation
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** All functionality accessible via keyboard
- **Do:** Tab order matches visual order
- **Don't:** Keyboard traps or illogical tab order
- **Code Example Good:** tabIndex for custom order
- **Code Example Bad:** Unreachable elements
- **Severity:** High

### Result 2
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Skip Links
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** Allow keyboard users to skip navigation
- **Do:** Provide skip to main content link
- **Don't:** No skip link on nav-heavy pages
- **Code Example Good:** Skip to main content link
- **Code Example Bad:** 100 tabs to reach content
- **Severity:** Medium

### Result 3
- **Category:** Accessibility
- **Issue:** Heading Hierarchy
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** Screen readers use headings for navigation
- **Do:** Use sequential heading levels h1-h6
- **Don't:** Skip heading levels or misuse for styling
- **Code Example Good:** h1 then h2 then h3
- **Code Example Bad:** h1 then h4
- **Severity:** Medium

### Result 4
- **Category:** Navigation
- **Issue:** Sticky Navigation
- **Platform:** Web
- **Description:** Fixed nav should not obscure content
- **Do:** Add padding-top to body equal to nav height
- **Don't:** Let nav overlap first section content
- **Code Example Good:** pt-20 (if nav is h-20)
- **Code Example Bad:** No padding compensation
- **Severity:** Medium

### Result 5
- **Category:** Interaction
- **Issue:** Focus States
- **Platform:** All
- **Description:** Keyboard users need visible focus indicators
- **Do:** Use visible focus rings on interactive elements
- **Don't:** Remove focus outline without replacement
- **Code Example Good:** focus:ring-2 focus:ring-blue-500
- **Code Example Bad:** outline-none without alternative
- **Severity:** High
```
</details></tool-use>

- **Severity:** Medium

- **Platform:** All

- **Don't:** No confirmation of completed action
- **Code Example Good:** Toast notification or checkmark
- **Code Example Bad:** Action completes silently
- **Severity:** Medium

- **Platform:** All

- **Do:** Add pressed/active state visual change
- **Don't:** No feedback during interaction

- **Severity:** Medium
